configurations.name: DD_AAS_JAVA_EXTENSION_VERSION
configurations.description: Azure App Services: sets the Datadog Java site extension version string used for the `aas.environment.extension_version` tag. If not set, the tracer reports `unknown`.

configurations.name: DD_ACTION_EXECUTION_ID
configurations.description: Sets the action execution ID reported as CI metadata for AWS CodePipeline builds. This value is used as the CI job identifier when running a CodeBuild job initiated by CodePipeline.

configurations.name: DD_AGENTLESS_LOG_SUBMISSION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables direct log submission to Datadog without routing logs through the Datadog Agent.

configurations.name: DD_AGENTLESS_LOG_SUBMISSION_LEVEL
configurations.description: Sets log level for Agentless submission

configurations.name: DD_AGENTLESS_LOG_SUBMISSION_QUEUE_SIZE
configurations.description: Sets the maximum size of pending logs queue

configurations.name: DD_AGENTLESS_LOG_SUBMISSION_URL
configurations.description: Overrides the intake URL used when agentless log submission is enabled.

configurations.name: DD_AGENT_HOST
configurations.description: Override the default trace Agent host address for trace submission. Ignored if `DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL` is set.

configurations.name: DD_AGENT_PORT
configurations.description: Overrides the port that the default tracer submit traces to. If the Agent configuration sets `receiver_port` or `DD_APM_RECEIVER_PORT` to something other than the default `8126`, then `DD_AGENT_PORT` or `DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL` must match it.

configurations.name: DD_AI_GUARD_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables AI Guard request inspection in the tracer; when disabled, AI Guard checks are skipped.

configurations.name: DD_AI_GUARD_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: AI Guard: sets the base endpoint URL for the AI Guard REST API. If unset, defaults to `https://app.<DD_SITE>/api/v2/ai-guard` (the tracer appends `/evaluate`).

configurations.name: DD_AI_GUARD_MAX_CONTENT_SIZE
configurations.description: Max size of the content property set in the meta-struct

configurations.name: DD_AI_GUARD_MAX_MESSAGES_LENGTH
configurations.description: Maximum number of conversational messages allowed to be set in the meta-struct

configurations.name: DD_AI_GUARD_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Timeout used in calls to the AI Guard REST API in milliseconds (default 5000)

configurations.name: DD_API_KEY
configurations.description: To use the Datadog CLI in Azure Cloud Shell, open a cloud shell, set your API key and site in the `DD_API_KEY` and `DD_SITE` environment variables, and use `npx` to run the CLI directly:

configurations.name: DD_API_KEY_FILE
configurations.description: Path to a file containing the Datadog API key. When set, the tracer reads the API key from this file (UTF-8, trimmed) instead of relying on `DD_API_KEY` alone; read errors are logged and the key is skipped.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_DOWNSTREAM_REQUEST_ANALYSIS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Legacy/alternate name for the API Security downstream request body analysis sampling rate. Sets a 0.0-1.0 sample rate used to decide which downstream (HTTP client) request bodies are analyzed for API Security.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_DOWNSTREAM_REQUEST_BODY_ANALYSIS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Defines the probability of a downstream request body being sampled, or said differently, defines the overall number of requests for which the request and response body should be sampled / analysed.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether API Security features are enabled. If unset, API Security is enabled by default.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_ENDPOINT_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether API endpoint definitions are collected for API catalog features. If unset, endpoint collection is enabled by default.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_ENDPOINT_COLLECTION_MESSAGE_LIMIT
configurations.description: Sets the maximum number of API endpoints included in a single endpoint-collection telemetry message. If unset, a default limit is used.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_MAX_DOWNSTREAM_REQUEST_BODY_ANALYSIS
configurations.description: The maximum number of downstream requests per request for which the request and response body should be analysed.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_SAMPLE_DELAY
configurations.description: Sets the delay (in seconds) between API Security schema samples. This setting is intended for system testing and is not meant for general use.

configurations.name: DD_APM_TRACING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables sending APM trace data. When disabled, tracing still runs to support other products, but only periodic heartbeat traces and manually kept traces are sent.

configurations.name: DD_APPLICATION_KEY
configurations.description: Your Datadog application key. This key, created by your Datadog organization, should include the `code_analysis_read` scope and be stored as a secret.

configurations.name: DD_APPLICATION_KEY_FILE
configurations.description: Path to a file containing the Datadog application key. When set, the tracer reads the application key from this file (UTF-8, trimmed) instead of relying on `DD_APP_KEY` / `DD_APPLICATION_KEY` alone; read errors are logged and the key is skipped.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_AUTOMATED_USER_EVENTS_TRACKING
configurations.description: null

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_AUTOMATED_USER_EVENTS_TRACKING
configurations.description: Controls automated user event tracking mode used by AppSec when `DD_APPSEC_AUTO_USER_INSTRUMENTATION_MODE` is not set. Values like `safe` map to anonymization, `extended` maps to identification, and other values disable automated user events.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_AUTO_USER_INSTRUMENTATION_MODE
configurations.description: Sets AppSec automated user instrumentation mode (user ID collection mode). Supported values include `identification`/`ident`, `anonymization`/`anon`, and `disabled`.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_BODY_PARSING_SIZE_LIMIT
configurations.description: Maximum size of the bodies to be processed in bytes. If set to `0`, the bodies are not processed. (Recommended value: `10000000` (10MB))

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables the AppSec product inside the tracer. When enabled the tracer initializes the AppSec module (in-app WAF, header/body collection and AppSec telemetry)

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HTTP_BLOCKED_TEMPLATE_HTML
configurations.description: Path to a local HTML template file that will be returned when a request is blocked and the response should be HTML. If unset or invalid, the tracer uses the default blocking HTML template defined by the product RFC. The tracer decides HTML or JSON according to `Accept` header or the block action's parameters.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HTTP_BLOCKED_TEMPLATE_JSON
configurations.description: Path to a local JSON template file that will be returned when a request is blocked and the response should be JSON. If unset or invalid, the tracer uses the default blocking HTML template defined by the product RFC. The tracer decides HTML or JSON according to `Accept` header or the block action's parameters.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_IPHEADER
configurations.description: Specify a single HTTP header to use for client IP resolution. When set, the tracer treats that header as the authoritative source of client IP and ignores the default set of candidate headers. This helps avoid ambiguity and IP spoofing issues when proxies or CDNs provide custom client IP headers; use with caution and follow deployment best practices.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_MAX_STACKTRACES
configurations.description: Legacy name for the AppSec max stack traces setting. See DD_APPSEC_MAX_STACK_TRACES

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_MAX_STACKTRACE_DEPTH
configurations.description: Legacy/alternate name for the AppSec max stack trace depth setting. See DD_APPSEC_MAX_STACK_TRACE_DEPTH

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_MAX_STACK_TRACES
configurations.description: Limits how many separate stacktraces are captured and attached to an AppSec/IAST event to keep payload sizes reasonable. Evidence stacktraces are useful for triage, but unbounded capture can leak sensitive data and consume excessive bandwidth; this limit balances evidence value and safety. Set to 0 to collect all.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_MAX_STACK_TRACE_DEPTH
configurations.description: Sets the maximum number of stack frames retained for any captured stacktrace attached to AppSec events. Tracers should prioritise top-of-stack frames and truncate deeper frames when this limit is reached. The setting helps keep evidentiary stacktraces compact while preserving the most actionable frames

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_OBFUSCATION_PARAMETER_KEY_REGEXP
configurations.description: A regular expression used by the AppSec obfuscator to identify parameter keys that must cause full redaction. When any `key_path` string matches this regexp the entire parameter is replaced by `<redacted by datadog>`. The default key regexp matches common secret/key names (password, token, authorization, jwt, etc.) an empty string disables the key regexp

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_OBFUSCATION_PARAMETER_VALUE_REGEXP
configurations.description: Regular expression used to find and redact sensitive substrings inside parameter values and highlights. Matches are replaced with `<redacted by datadog>`. This regexp complements the key regexp: the key regexp takes precedence; when no key match exists, the value regexp runs. The default pattern targeted at tokens, JWTs, private key blocks, and other credential shapes. Empty string disables the value regexp

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_RASP_ENABLED
configurations.description: Turns on the tracer's RASP / Exploit Prevention capabilities. When enabled the tracer runs synchronous, pre-execution checks and may generate block actions or exploit signals. RASP may have separate activation and implementation details per language; enabling it usually activates extra synchronous instrumentation and additional evidence capture for detections

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_REPORTING_INBAND
configurations.description: null

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_REPORT_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: null

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_RULES
configurations.description: Override the default rules file provided. Must be a path to a valid JSON rules file

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_SCA_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the tracer's runtime Software Composition Analysis (Runtime SCA) capability, which reports runtime dependency/SBOM information and powers runtime vulnerability detection. Useful for testing and demonstrations as well as production runtime vulnerability workflows; SCA may have billing/usage implications and language compatibility constraints described in the SCA runbooks

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_STACKTRACE_ENABLED
configurations.description: See `DD_APPSEC_STACK_TRACE_ENABLED`

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_STACK_TRACE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables security-related stack traces generation when security events occur.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_TRACE_RATE_LIMIT
configurations.description: Controls the maximum amount of AppSec traces, per second.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_WAF_METRICS
configurations.description: Enables AppSec WAF metrics reporting. When enabled, the tracer adds WAF statistics reporting (for example, via the WAF stats reporter); when disabled, WAF metrics are not reported.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_WAF_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Limits the WAF synchronous execution time (in microseconds).

configurations.name: DD_APP_CUSTOMJMXBUILDER
configurations.description: Indicates the application uses a custom JMX `MBeanServerBuilder` (`javax.management.builder.initial`). When set to `true`, the tracer delays starting JMXFetch (and other JMX-dependent startup work) until the custom builder is initialized, to avoid interfering with application JMX initialization. Setting it to `false` disables this automatic delay/detection.

configurations.name: DD_APP_CUSTOMLOGMANAGER
configurations.description: Indicates the application uses a custom Java Util Logging (JUL) `LogManager` (`java.util.logging.manager`). When set to `true`, the tracer delays JMXFetch and tracer/profiling initialization until JUL is initialized, to avoid preventing the application from installing its custom log manager. Setting it to `false` disables this automatic delay/detection.

configurations.name: DD_APP_LOGS_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the collection of application logs

configurations.name: DD_AWS_PROPAGATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: null

configurations.name: DD_AWS_SDK_PROPAGATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: null

configurations.name: DD_AZURE_APP_SERVICES
configurations.description: Enables Azure App Services mode. When enabled, the tracer adds Azure App Services environment tags (`aas.*`) to the local root span and profiling tags (derived from Azure environment variables such as `WEBSITE_*` and `DD_AAS_JAVA_EXTENSION_VERSION`).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_ADDITIONAL_CHILD_PROCESS_JVM_ARGS
configurations.description: Extra JVM arguments appended when CI Visibility auto-configures child JVM processes (for example, Maven/Gradle forked test JVMs) that are started with the tracer attached.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_AGENTLESS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Agentless mode in CI Visibility. Default value is false (disabled).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_AGENTLESS_URL
configurations.description: Overrides the intake base URL used for agentless CI test reporting. When set, payloads are sent to this URL instead of the default site-based intake URL.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_AGENT_JAR_URI
configurations.description: URI to the Java tracer `-javaagent` JAR used by CI Visibility auto-configuration to inject the tracer into child JVM processes (for example, Maven/Gradle test JVMs). This value is converted to a file path and used to build the `-javaagent:<path>` argument.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_AUTO_CONFIGURATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables CI Visibility auto-configuration for build systems. When disabled, the Maven/Gradle configurators do not inject `-javaagent` into forked test JVMs and do not apply automatic CI Visibility configuration.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_AUTO_INSTRUMENTATION_PROVIDER
configurations.description: Marks CI test sessions as running under an auto-instrumentation workflow. Any non-empty value enables this marker for telemetry.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_BACKEND_API_TIMEOUT_MILLIS
configurations.description: Timeout (milliseconds) for HTTP calls to CI Visibility backend APIs. When CI Visibility is enabled, this value is used as the shared HTTP client timeout instead of the generic agent timeout.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_BUILD_INSTRUMENTATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables CI Visibility build system instrumentation (for example, Maven/Gradle build instrumentation). When disabled, CI Visibility build instrumentations are not applied.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_CIPROVIDER_INTEGRATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables CI provider integration for CI Visibility. When disabled, the tracer does not try to detect Jenkins/GitLab/GitHub Actions/etc from environment variables and uses an "unknown" CI provider.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_CODE_COVERAGE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables CI Visibility per-test code coverage collection/reporting. When enabled, the tracer configures an additional intake track (`CITESTCOV`) to submit coverage data; when disabled, code coverage data is not sent.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_CODE_COVERAGE_EXCLUDES
configurations.description: Colon-separated list of package patterns to exclude from CI Visibility code coverage (JaCoCo exclusion format). These patterns are split on `:` and used to build excluded package prefixes.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_CODE_COVERAGE_INCLUDES
configurations.description: Colon-separated list of package patterns to include for CI Visibility code coverage (JaCoCo inclusion format). If set and non-empty, it overrides auto-detected repository root packages; otherwise, root packages are derived from the repo index.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_CODE_COVERAGE_LINES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables line-level (probe-based) code coverage instrumentation for CI Visibility (JaCoCo). When enabled, the tracer applies JaCoCo agent instrumentations needed for per-test line granularity.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_CODE_COVERAGE_REPORT_DUMP_DIR
configurations.description: Directory where CI Visibility dumps generated aggregated JaCoCo coverage reports (HTML and `jacoco.xml`). When set, reports are written under `<dumpDir>/(session|module)-<eventId>/aggregated`.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_CODE_COVERAGE_REPORT_UPLOAD_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether CI Visibility uploads aggregated code coverage reports to Datadog. This value is used as a local default/fallback for execution settings (remote CI Visibility settings may override it).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_CODE_COVERAGE_ROOT_PACKAGES_LIMIT
configurations.description: Maximum number of root packages to keep when deriving the repository package list for CI Visibility code coverage. If there are more packages than the limit, the package list is coarsened/truncated to fit.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_COMPILER_PLUGIN_AUTO_CONFIGURATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables automatic configuration of the Datadog Java compiler plugin (dd-javac-plugin) for CI Visibility in supported build tools (for example, Maven/Gradle). When disabled, the tracer does not auto-configure the compiler plugin.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_COMPILER_PLUGIN_VERSION
configurations.description: Version of the Datadog Java compiler plugin (dd-javac-plugin and dd-javac-plugin-client) to use when CI Visibility auto-configures compilation instrumentation in supported build tools.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_DEBUG_PORT
configurations.description: When CI Visibility auto-configures forked JVM processes, sets the JDWP debug port and adds a `-agentlib:jdwp=...address=<port>` argument (suspending the child JVM waiting for a debugger connection).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_EARLY_FLAKE_DETECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables CI Visibility Early Flake Detection (EFD), which can run selected tests multiple times early to detect flakiness (based on backend-provided execution settings).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_EARLY_FLAKE_DETECTION_LOWER_LIMIT
configurations.description: Lower limit threshold for CI Visibility Early Flake Detection (EFD). This value is sent/propagated in build execution settings and used to determine EFD behavior thresholds.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enabling or disabling CI Visibility

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_EXECUTION_SETTINGS_CACHE_SIZE
configurations.description: Cache size used by CI Visibility to cache execution settings and JVM info lookups (fixed-size cache for settings per JVM / JVM executable path).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_FLAKY_RETRY_COUNT
configurations.description: Can be set to any non-negative number to change the maximum number of retries per test case.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_FLAKY_RETRY_ENABLED
configurations.description: A kill switch for the auto test retries feature.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_FLAKY_RETRY_ONLY_KNOWN_FLAKES
configurations.description: When CI Visibility auto test retries are enabled, restricts fetching flaky tests data to only "known" flakes (otherwise flaky tests data may be skipped unless fail-fast ordering is used).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_GIT_CLIENT_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables using the local `git` executable to collect Git metadata for CI Visibility. When disabled, a no-op Git client is used and Git data collection features are unavailable.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_GIT_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_MILLIS
configurations.description: Timeout (milliseconds) for running `git` commands when CI Visibility collects Git metadata using the shell Git client.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_GIT_REMOTE_NAME
configurations.description: Git remote name (for example, `origin`) used by CI Visibility when resolving the repository URL for Git metadata collection and Git data upload.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_GIT_UNSHALLOW_DEFER
configurations.description: Controls whether CI Visibility defers `git unshallow` until it knows Git data upload is needed. When false, the repo is unshallowed before collecting Git info; when true, unshallow may be performed later only if required.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_GIT_UNSHALLOW_ENABLED
configurations.description: Provide users the option to enable/disable the `git unshallow` process wheen retrieving git metadata.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_GIT_UPLOAD_ENABLED
configurations.description: Disable git upload if the DD_CIVISIBILITY_GIT_UPLOAD_ENABLED is set to false

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_GIT_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT_MILLIS
configurations.description: Timeout (milliseconds) to wait for CI Visibility Git data upload to finish before proceeding with backend configuration/settings requests.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_GRADLE_SOURCESETS
configurations.description: List of Gradle source set names (default: `main`, `test`) used by CI Visibility when selecting which source sets to consider for coverage/module layout in Gradle builds.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_IMPACTED_TESTS_DETECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables CI Visibility impacted tests detection (TIA). When enabled, impacted-tests detection is requested/applied as part of execution settings propagated to child processes.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_INJECTED_TRACER_VERSION
configurations.description: Tracer version string propagated from a parent process when it auto-injects the tracer into child JVMs. Used to detect mismatched tracer versions and to avoid instrumenting nested build JVMs.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_INTAKE_AGENTLESS_URL
configurations.description: Custom base URL for the CI Intake in CI Visibility agentless mode. When set, CI events are sent to `<url>/api/v2/` for the `ci-intake` endpoint (instead of `https://ci-intake.<DD_SITE>/api/v2/`).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_ITR_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Intelligent Test Runner in CI Visibility. Default Value is false (disabled).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_JACOCO_PLUGIN_VERSION
configurations.description: JaCoCo plugin version to use when CI Visibility injects/configures JaCoCo for coverage (for example, injected `jacoco-maven-plugin` or Gradle JaCoCo tool version).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_JVM_INFO_CACHE_SIZE
configurations.description: Cache size for CI Visibility JVM runtime info lookups (cached by JVM executable path).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_KNOWN_TESTS_REQUEST_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables requesting "known tests" data from the backend as part of CI Visibility execution settings (used for known-tests marking and related behaviors).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_MODULE_NAME
configurations.description: CI Visibility: sets the module name used for module-scoped execution settings and tags. In child/forked JVMs, this is typically propagated from the parent build process; when set, it overrides deriving the module name from the repository path or the service name.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_REMOTE_ENV_VARS_PROVIDER_KEY
configurations.description: CI Visibility: sets the provider key sent as the `DD-Env-Vars-Provider-Key` HTTP header when fetching remote environment variables from `DD_CIVISIBILITY_REMOTE_ENV_VARS_PROVIDER_URL`.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_REMOTE_ENV_VARS_PROVIDER_URL
configurations.description: CI Visibility: sets the URL to fetch remote environment variables from (HTTP GET). When set together with `DD_CIVISIBILITY_REMOTE_ENV_VARS_PROVIDER_KEY`, the tracer loads the returned Java properties (env-var keys) and uses them as an additional environment/config source (for example, for CI provider detection).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_REPO_INDEX_DUPLICATE_KEY_CHECK_ENABLED
configurations.description: CI Visibility repo index: when enabled (default), source path resolution fails if the repo index contains multiple entries for the same key (to avoid ambiguous source mapping). When disabled, duplicate index keys are tolerated.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_REPO_INDEX_FOLLOW_SYMLINKS
configurations.description: CI Visibility repo index: controls whether symlink directories are traversed while building the repository index. When disabled (default), symlink directories are skipped; when enabled, symlinks may be visited (with safeguards to avoid duplicate results for links pointing inside the repo).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_RESOURCE_FOLDER_NAMES
configurations.description: CI Visibility source indexing: list of conventional resource-folder path segments used to resolve a resource root from an absolute resource file path (naive substring match). Defaults to `/resources/`, `/java/`, `/groovy/`, `/kotlin/`, `/scala/`.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_RUM_FLUSH_WAIT_MILLIS
configurations.description: CI Visibility (Selenium): after calling `window.DD_RUM.stopSession()` at the end of a browser test, waits this many milliseconds before proceeding to allow RUM data to flush (default: 500ms).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_SCALATEST_FORK_MONITOR_ENABLED
configurations.description: CI Visibility (Scalatest/SBT): enables the Scalatest fork monitor used when SBT runs tests with forking (`Test / fork`), to avoid double-reporting by suppressing Scalatest tracing in the parent process and only instrumenting the forked test JVM (default: false).

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_SIGNAL_CLIENT_TIMEOUT_MILLIS
configurations.description: CI Visibility IPC: socket timeout (milliseconds) for the Signal client used by child JVMs to connect to the parent process Signal server (applies to connect and read). Default: 10000ms.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_SIGNAL_SERVER_HOST
configurations.description: CI Visibility IPC: host/address that the parent process Signal server binds to (and that child JVMs use to connect back). Default: `127.0.0.1`.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_SIGNAL_SERVER_PORT
configurations.description: CI Visibility IPC: port that the parent process Signal server binds to (0 selects an ephemeral port). Child JVMs connect to this port to send signals/results back to the parent.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_SOURCE_DATA_ENABLED
configurations.description: CI Visibility: enables attaching source code metadata to test and test-suite spans (for example, `test.source.file`, start/end line numbers, and CODEOWNERS). Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_TELEMETRY_ENABLED
configurations.description: CI Visibility: enables CI Visibility telemetry (collects CI Visibility metrics and adds a trace interceptor that reports CI Visibility trace event counts). Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_TEST_COMMAND
configurations.description: CI Visibility: sets the test command string propagated to child test JVMs (for example, `mvn test` or `gradle test`). It is used to build the CI Visibility test session name when an explicit session name is not set.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_TEST_ORDER
configurations.description: CI Visibility: configures test execution ordering instrumentation. Currently supports `FAILFAST` to enable fail-fast ordering (for example, installs JUnit 5 class/method orderers); other values are treated as unknown/invalid by the instrumentation.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_TEST_SKIPPING_ENABLED
configurations.description: CI Visibility: enables automatic test skipping in instrumented test frameworks (for example, JUnit/TestNG). When enabled, the tracer may mark tests as skipped/ignored based on backend-provided execution settings (for example, Intelligent Test Runner). Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_TOTAL_FLAKY_RETRY_COUNT
configurations.description: Sets the maximum total number of automatic test retries allowed across the entire test session. This limit is applied when automatic flaky-test retries are enabled and defaults to 1000.

configurations.name: DD_CIVISIBILITY_TRACE_SANITATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: CI Visibility: when enabled (default), applies a trace interceptor that drops non-CI Visibility traces (root span origin is not `ciapp-test`) and sets `library_version` on CI Visibility spans.

configurations.name: DD_CODE_ORIGIN_FOR_SPANS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Code Origin for spans (Dynamic Instrumentation): starts the code-origin recorder and enables instrumentations that capture code origin at span entry (via `DebuggerContext.captureCodeOrigin`), producing code-origin snapshots/tags for spans. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_CODE_ORIGIN_MAX_USER_FRAMES
configurations.description: Code Origin for spans (Dynamic Instrumentation): sets the maximum number of user stack frames to capture/consider when recording code origin (default: 8). Note: the current code-origin recorder reads this value but does not use it, so it may have no effect in this version.

configurations.name: DD_CRASHTRACKING_AGENTLESS
configurations.description: Crash Tracking (not intended for production use): when enabled, the tracer uploads crash telemetry and error-tracking payloads directly to Datadog intake (based on `DD_SITE`) instead of proxying through the local Datadog Agent. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_CRASHTRACKING_DEBUG_AUTOCONFIG_ENABLE
configurations.description: Crash Tracking (debug): enables crash tracking auto-configuration (JVM hooks). When enabled, crash tracking attempts to auto-configure `-XX:OnError` / `-XX:OnOutOfMemoryError` via the native JVM-access library; when disabled, crash tracking may defer initialization and rely on user-provided JVM flags. If unset, this defaults to whether profiling is enabled.

configurations.name: DD_CRASHTRACKING_DEBUG_START_FORCE_FIRST
configurations.description: Crash Tracking (debug): forces crash tracking initialization to run immediately at startup instead of being deferred to a background task / after JMX. This can add startup overhead (native library init can take 100ms+). Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_CRASHTRACKING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Crash Tracking. When enabled, the tracer initializes crash tracking and configures JVM crash/OOM hooks (for example, `-XX:OnError` and `-XX:OnOutOfMemoryError`) to run Datadog-provided scripts that upload `hs_err_pid*.log` crash logs and OOME notifications. Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_CRASHTRACKING_ERRORS_INTAKE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enable sending the crash to the error tracking intake

configurations.name: DD_CRASHTRACKING_PROXY_HOST
configurations.description: Crash Tracking: proxy host for the crash uploader HTTP client (used to send crash telemetry and error-tracking payloads).

configurations.name: DD_CRASHTRACKING_PROXY_PASSWORD
configurations.description: Crash Tracking: proxy password for the crash uploader HTTP client (used with `DD_CRASHTRACKING_PROXY_USERNAME`).

configurations.name: DD_CRASHTRACKING_PROXY_PORT
configurations.description: Crash Tracking: proxy port for the crash uploader HTTP client.

configurations.name: DD_CRASHTRACKING_PROXY_USERNAME
configurations.description: Crash Tracking: proxy username for the crash uploader HTTP client (used with `DD_CRASHTRACKING_PROXY_PASSWORD`).

configurations.name: DD_CRASHTRACKING_TAGS
configurations.description: Crash Tracking: additional tags to attach to crash reports (merged with global/JMX/runtime tags, and includes service/env/version/language/host).

configurations.name: DD_CRASHTRACKING_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Crash Tracking: upload timeout in seconds for crash uploader HTTP requests. Default: 2 seconds.

configurations.name: DD_CUSTOM_TRACE_ID
configurations.description: Custom variable set by the Jenkins Datadog Plugin for trace IDs.

configurations.name: DD_CWS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Cloud Workload Security (CWS). When enabled, the tracer registers a scope listener that writes the active trace/span IDs into thread-local storage for the CWS eBPF side to read. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_CWS_TLS_REFRESH
configurations.description: CWS TLS: refresh interval in milliseconds for re-registering the thread-local storage pointer with the CWS eRPC layer (background thread periodically calls `registerTls()` and sleeps `refresh`). Default: 5000ms.

configurations.name: DD_DATA_JOBS_COMMAND_PATTERN
configurations.description: Data Jobs Monitoring: regex pattern matched against the current JVM command line. When Data Jobs Monitoring is enabled and the command does not match this pattern, the tracer aborts installation; invalid regex values are ignored with a warning.

configurations.name: DD_DATA_JOBS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Data Jobs Monitoring. When enabled, the tracer auto-enables Spark/Spark-executor integrations, long-running traces, and enables Data Streams Monitoring by default; it also validates the JVM command against `DD_DATA_JOBS_COMMAND_PATTERN` and can abort tracer installation if incompatible.

configurations.name: DD_DATA_JOBS_EXPERIMENTAL_FEATURES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Data Jobs Monitoring: enables experimental Spark instrumentation features. When enabled, Spark instrumentation may extract and attach additional Spark plan metadata (for example, when `SparkPlanInfo` metadata is empty). Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_DATA_JOBS_OPENLINEAGE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Data Jobs Monitoring: enables OpenLineage support for Spark by injecting the `io.openlineage.spark.agent.OpenLineageSparkListener` into `spark.extraListeners` (when the OpenLineage Spark agent is present). Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_DATA_JOBS_OPENLINEAGE_TIMEOUT_ENABLED
configurations.description: Data Jobs Monitoring (OpenLineage): when enabled (default), configures a timeout circuit breaker in Spark OpenLineage settings (`spark.openlineage.circuitBreaker.type=timeout`) if supported and not already configured, to avoid OpenLineage operations hanging.

configurations.name: DD_DATA_JOBS_PARSE_SPARK_PLAN_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables parsing of the Spark Plan for additional metadata details

configurations.name: DD_DATA_STREAMS_BUCKET_DURATION_SECONDS
configurations.description: Data Streams Monitoring: bucket/flush duration in seconds (aggregation window). Controls how often DSM payloads are reported (scheduled at this interval). Default: 10 seconds.

configurations.name: DD_DATA_STREAMS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Data Streams Monitoring (DSM) and propagation. When enabled, the tracer registers the DSM propagator and reports pathway stats/payloads. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_DATA_STREAMS_TRANSACTION_EXTRACTORS
configurations.description: Allows configuring Data Streams extractors for transaction tracking

configurations.name: DD_DBM_ALWAYS_APPEND_SQL_COMMENT
configurations.description: When the `DD_DBM_PROPAGATION_MODE` is set, a comment is injected in SQL queries. Most of the time it is prepended. This config forces the comment to be appended at all times.

configurations.name: DD_DBM_INJECT_SQL_BASEHASH
configurations.description: Enables the injection of a "base hash" in SQL queries when DBM propagation is also enabled. This base hash is used to find matching spans, and enrich the queries with related values.

configurations.name: DD_DBM_PROPAGATION_MODE
configurations.description: Enables linking between data sent from APM and the Database Monitoring product when set to `service` or `full`. The `service` option enables the connection between DBM and APM services. The `full` option enables connection between database spans with database query events. Available for Postgres and MySQL.

configurations.name: DD_DBM_TRACE_PREPARED_STATEMENTS
configurations.description: Database Monitoring (DBM): when enabled, Postgres prepared statements propagate trace context by setting `application_name` (via `Connection#setClientInfo`) because comments cannot be used, at the cost of an extra round trip. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_DETECT_AOT_TRAINING
configurations.description: TO BE DELETED

configurations.name: DD_DETECT_AOT_TRAINING_MODE
configurations.description: Temporary internal flag in case we need to roll-back detection of the JVM's AOT training mode

configurations.name: DD_DISTRIBUTED_DEBUGGER_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Distributed Debugger. When disabled (default), "trigger" probes are not installed by the dynamic instrumentation transformer.

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_ARGS
configurations.description: Azure App Services: extra command-line arguments appended when the tracer starts an external DogStatsD process (only used when `DD_DOGSTATSD_PATH` is set).

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_HOST
configurations.description: Override the address of the trace Agent host that the default tracer attempts to submit DogStatsD metrics to. Use `DD_AGENT_HOST` to override `DD_DOGSTATSD_HOST`.

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_PATH
configurations.description: Azure App Services: path to the DogStatsD executable to run as an external process. When set, the tracer starts and supervises a `dogstatsd` process (passing `DD_DOGSTATSD_ARGS`) and optionally health-checks it using `DD_DOGSTATSD_PIPE_NAME`.

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_PIPE_NAME
configurations.description: Named pipe that DogStatsD binds to. Default value is null.

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_PORT
configurations.description: The port of the DogStatsD Agent that metrics are submitted to. If the Agent configuration sets `dogstatsd_port` or `DD_DOGSTATSD_PORT` to something other than the default `8125`, then this tracing library `DD_DOGSTATSD_PORT` must match it.

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_START_DELAY
configurations.description: DogStatsD client start delay (seconds). Delays establishing the DogStatsD/StatsD connection until this many seconds after tracer start time (default: 15).

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_CAPTURE_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Timeout (milliseconds) used when capturing/freezing context for snapshots and serializing captured values

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_CLASSFILE_DUMP_ENABLED
configurations.description: When enabled, dumps original and instrumented `.class` files to disk during transformation (for debugging live instrumentation)

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_DIAGNOSTICS_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Interval (seconds) at which probe diagnostic/status messages (received/installed/emitting/errors) may be re-emitted

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables Dynamic Instrumentation.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_EXCLUDE_FILES
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of file paths containing exclude rules (class/package prefixes ending with `*`, fully-qualified classes, or `Class::method` entries)

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_INCLUDE_FILES
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of file paths containing include rules (same format as exclude files)

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_INSTRUMENT_THE_WORLD
configurations.description: Enables broad instrumentation mode. Valid values are method or line, which controls what probes are generated. When active, uploads/diagnostics are suppressed and include/exclude files are used to scope instrumentation.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_LOCALVAR_HOISTING_LEVEL
configurations.description: Local variable hoisting level used when instrumenting methods for captured context. 0 disables hoisting; higher levels hoist more locals to the top of the method.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE
configurations.description: Maximum size in bytes of a single configuration payload that can be handled per request.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_METRICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables diagnostic metrics reporting for the debugger agent.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_POLL_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Poll interval (seconds) for configuration updates.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_PROBE_FILE
configurations.description: Path to a local probe definition JSON file. When set, the tracer loads probe definitions from this file at startup instead of subscribing to remote configuration.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_REDACTED_IDENTIFIERS
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of additional identifier keywords to redact in captured snapshot data

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_REDACTED_TYPES
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of object types to redact from dynamic logs and snapshots. Supports * wildcards (e.g. Secret* matches SecretKey).

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_REDACTION_EXCLUDED_IDENTIFIERS
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of identifier keywords to exclude from the default redaction list. Useful to prevent common keywords like password from being treated as sensitive for your use case.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_SNAPSHOT_URL
configurations.description: Overrides the debugger snapshot/log intake URL used by the tracer. When set, it replaces the default debugger diagnostics URL

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_SOURCE_FILE_TRACKING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables source-file tracking. When enabled (default), installs a transformer that maps source files to loaded classes so retransformation of all classes associated with a source file is possible.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_UPLOAD_BATCH_SIZE
configurations.description: Upload batch size (number of snapshots/diagnostic messages per request). Controls how many snapshots are serialized and sent per flush. Default: 100.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_UPLOAD_FLUSH_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Low-rate upload flush interval in milliseconds. 0 (default) enables an adaptive flush interval; any other value uses a fixed interval. Deprecated in favor of DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_UPLOAD_INTERVAL_SECONDS.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_UPLOAD_INTERVAL_SECONDS
configurations.description: Interval in seconds between uploads of probe data.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds for uploads (snapshots/logs/diagnostics).

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_VERIFY_BYTECODE
configurations.description: When enabled (default), verifies generated instrumented bytecode and throws if verification fails.

configurations.name: DD_ENV
configurations.description: Sets the global `env` for all data emitted by the Agent. If `env` is not present in your trace data, this variable is used.

configurations.name: DD_EXCEPTION_DEBUGGING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Exception Replay (Live Debugging): enables exception debugging at startup. When enabled, the tracer starts the Exception Replay subsystem which installs exception probes and captures snapshots for handled exceptions. Default: false. Note: `DD_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_ENABLED` is also accepted as a backward-compatible alias.

configurations.name: DD_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_CAPTURE_INTERMEDIATE_SPANS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Exception Replay: when enabled (default), allows Exception Replay to handle exceptions on non-root spans as well as local root spans. When disabled, exceptions are captured only on local root spans (unless overridden by other root-only settings). Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_CAPTURE_INTERVAL_SECONDS
configurations.description: Exception Replay: minimum interval in seconds between capturing the same exception fingerprint again. Used to rate-limit repeated captures of identical exceptions. Default: 3600 seconds.

configurations.name: DD_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_CAPTURE_MAX_FRAMES
configurations.description: Exception Replay (legacy): deprecated alias for `DD_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_MAX_FRAMES_TO_CAPTURE`. Sets the maximum number of stack frames (non-native, non-excluded, with line numbers) to instrument/capture per exception. Default: 3.

configurations.name: DD_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Exception Replay (Live Debugging): enables the Exception Replay product. When enabled (for example via Remote Config), the tracer starts/stops the Exception Replay subsystem. This key is also accepted as a backward-compatible alias for `DD_EXCEPTION_DEBUGGING_ENABLED`.

configurations.name: DD_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_MAX_EXCEPTION_ANALYSIS_LIMIT
configurations.description: Exception Replay: maximum number of exceptions per second to analyze/handle (circuit breaker). When the limit is exceeded, additional exceptions are ignored for that second. Default: 100.

configurations.name: DD_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_MAX_FRAMES_TO_CAPTURE
configurations.description: Exception Replay: maximum number of stack frames (non-native, non-excluded, with line numbers) to instrument/capture per exception when creating exception probes. Default: 3.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_API_SECURITY_ENABLED
configurations.description: API Security (experimental): enables API Security (deprecated experimental key; alias for `api-security.enabled`). When enabled and AppSec is active, the tracer initializes the API Security sampler and span post-processing needed for API Security features.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_DEFER_INTEGRATIONS_UNTIL
configurations.description: Instrumentation (experimental): defers integration matching/activation until a configured delay (parsed as a simple duration). When the delay is recognized and 5 seconds, deferred matching is resumed later via a scheduled task.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_FLAGGING_PROVIDER_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the experimental feature-flag provider that evaluates flags from remote configuration. If not enabled, the provider is a no-op and returns default values.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_PROPAGATE_PROCESS_TAGS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether process-level tags (such as entrypoint metadata) are collected and included in tracing metadata. If unset, this is enabled by default.

configurations.name: DD_FORCE_CLEAR_TEXT_HTTP_FOR_INTAKE_CLIENT
configurations.description: Forces the intake HTTP client to use cleartext (no TLS) connection specs. Intended for environments/JVMs without TLS support when communicating with intake over HTTP. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_BRANCH
configurations.description: Overrides the Git branch value used by CI Visibility for repository and commit correlation.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_AUTHOR_DATE
configurations.description: The date when the author submitted the commit expressed in ISO 8601 format.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit author email reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_AUTHOR_NAME
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit author name reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_COMMITTER_DATE
configurations.description: The date when the committer submitted the commit expressed in ISO 8601 format.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit committer email reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_COMMITTER_NAME
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit committer name reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_HEAD_SHA
configurations.description: CI Visibility: sets the git head commit SHA for the current build/pull request, used to populate pull request info and CI git tags (for example, `git.commit.head.sha`) when user-supplied git metadata is needed.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_MESSAGE
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit message reported as CI metadata.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_COMMIT_SHA
configurations.description: Overrides the git commit SHA reported as CI metadata and used for git metadata tagging when enabled.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_PULL_REQUEST_BASE_BRANCH
configurations.description: CI Visibility: sets the pull request base/target branch name, used to populate pull request info and CI git tags (for example, `git.pull_request.base_branch`) when user-supplied git metadata is needed.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_PULL_REQUEST_BASE_BRANCH_SHA
configurations.description: Overrides the pull request base commit SHA reported as git metadata for CI features. Use this to set the base commit when it cannot be detected automatically.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_REPOSITORY_URL
configurations.description: Overrides the git repository URL reported as CI metadata and used for git metadata tagging when enabled. Any embedded credentials in the URL are stripped before use.

configurations.name: DD_GIT_TAG
configurations.description: Overrides the Git tag used by CI Visibility for repository and release correlation.

configurations.name: DD_GRPC_CLIENT_ERROR_STATUSES
configurations.description: A range of errors can be accepted. By default, gRPC status codes 1 to 16 are reported as errors for gRPC clients. This configuration overrides that. Ex. `dd.grpc.client.error.statuses=1-4,7-10`

configurations.name: DD_GRPC_SERVER_ERROR_STATUSES
configurations.description: A range of errors can be accepted. By default, gRPC status codes 2 to 16 are reported as errors for gRPC servers. This configuration overrides that. Ex. `dd.grpc.server.error.statuses=2-4,7-10`

configurations.name: DD_HTTP_CLIENT_ERROR_STATUSES
configurations.description: Client HTTP status codes that mark spans as errors.

configurations.name: DD_HTTP_CLIENT_TAG_HEADERS
configurations.description: HTTP client: when enabled (default), tags HTTP client spans with configured request/response header values (from the tracer's header-to-tag mapping, e.g. `DD_TRACE_HEADER_TAGS`). When disabled, HTTP header tagging is skipped.

configurations.name: DD_HTTP_CLIENT_TAG_QUERY_STRING
configurations.description: By default, query string parameters and fragments are added to the `http.url` tag on web client spans. Set to `false` to prevent the collection of this data.

configurations.name: DD_HTTP_SERVER_DECODED_RESOURCE_PRESERVE_SPACES
configurations.description: HTTP server: controls whether spaces are preserved when normalizing decoded URL paths into resource names. When enabled (default), whitespace characters are kept; when disabled, whitespace is removed during normalization.

configurations.name: DD_HTTP_SERVER_ERROR_STATUSES
configurations.description: Server HTTP status codes that mark spans as errors.

configurations.name: DD_HTTP_SERVER_RAW_QUERY_STRING
configurations.description: HTTP server: when query-string tagging is enabled and raw URL components are available, uses the raw (percent-encoded) query string (`rawQuery`) instead of the decoded query string. Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_HTTP_SERVER_RAW_RESOURCE
configurations.description: HTTP server: when enabled and raw URL components are available, uses the raw path (`rawPath`) when tagging `http.url` and building the server resource name, preserving percent-encoding. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_HTTP_SERVER_ROUTE_BASED_NAMING
configurations.description: When set to `false` http framework routes are not used for resource names. _This can change resource names and derived metrics if changed._

configurations.name: DD_HTTP_SERVER_TAG_QUERY_STRING
configurations.description: Enables or disables reporting the query string on server spans. Default value is true.

configurations.name: DD_HYSTRIX_MEASURED_ENABLED
configurations.description: Hystrix: when enabled, marks Hystrix spans as measured (`span.setMeasured(true)`), so they contribute to trace metrics. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_HYSTRIX_TAGS_ENABLED
configurations.description: By default the Hystrix group, command, and circuit state tags are not enabled. This property enables them.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_ANONYMOUS_CLASSES_ENABLED
configurations.description: IAST: controls whether IAST call site instrumentation applies to anonymous classes (following the Java `$<digits>` naming convention). When disabled, anonymous classes are excluded from the IAST matcher. Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_CONTEXT_MODE
configurations.description: IAST: selects how IAST context is scoped: `REQUEST` (default) stores context on the active request span; `GLOBAL` uses a single global context instance. This also affects how IAST overhead controls are applied.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_DB_ROWS_TO_TAINT
configurations.description: IAST: maximum number of JDBC `ResultSet` rows to treat as taint sources (SQL table data) for a given `ResultSet`. Rows beyond this limit are not tainted. Default: 1.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_DEBUG_ENABLED
configurations.description: IAST: enables debug mode (extra debug logging/telemetry and a debug overhead-controller adapter). Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_DEDUPLICATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables vulnerability deduplication detection. When enabled, a vulnerability is only reported once in the lifetime of an app, instead of on every occurrence. Default value is true (enabled).

configurations.name: DD_IAST_DETECTION_MODE
configurations.description: IAST: sets detection mode. `DEFAULT` applies overhead controls (sampling, concurrency limits, deduplication, range limits). `FULL` removes these limits (100% sampling, unlimited concurrency/ranges) and enables additional full-detection call sites. Default: `DEFAULT`.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_ENABLED
configurations.description: IAST: controls IAST product activation. `true`/`1` fully enables IAST; `inactive` applies instrumentation but keeps logic inactive so it can be toggled via remote config; any other value fully disables IAST. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_EXPERIMENTAL_PROPAGATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: IAST: enables experimental taint-propagation call sites for additional `String` operations (for example, `String.replace*`). Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_HARDCODED_SECRET_ENABLED
configurations.description: IAST: enables hardcoded secret detection instrumentation (registers hardcoded-secret listener/call sites). Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_MAX_CONCURRENT_REQUESTS
configurations.description: IAST: maximum number of concurrent requests that can be analyzed (overhead control). Only used in `DEFAULT` detection mode; in `FULL` detection mode this is unlimited. Default (in `DEFAULT` mode): 4.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_MAX_RANGE_COUNT
configurations.description: IAST: maximum number of tainted ranges stored per tainted object/value. If more ranges are produced, they are truncated to this limit. Only used in `DEFAULT` detection mode; in `FULL` detection mode this is unlimited. Default (in `DEFAULT` mode): 10.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_REDACTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables IAST redaction of sensitive data. Default value is in TracerSettings.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_REDACTION_NAME_PATTERN
configurations.description: IAST: regular expression used to identify sensitive source names (header/parameter/cookie names, etc.) that should be redacted (compiled case-insensitively). Default matches common secrets like password/token/key.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_REDACTION_VALUE_PATTERN
configurations.description: IAST: regular expression used to identify sensitive values that should be redacted (compiled case-insensitively, multiline). Default matches patterns like Bearer tokens, GitHub/GitLab tokens, JWTs, and private keys.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_REQUEST_SAMPLING
configurations.description: IAST: request sampling percentage for analysis (overhead control). Used by the IAST overhead controller to decide which requests are analyzed. Values <= 0 are treated as 100% (it does not disable IAST). In `FULL` detection mode the sampling is forced to 100%. Default (in `DEFAULT` mode): 33%.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_SECURITY_CONTROLS_CONFIGURATION
configurations.description: IAST: configures custom security controls (sanitizers / input validators) via a semicolon-separated configuration string. When set, the tracer installs a transformer that instruments the specified classes/methods so IAST can mark data as validated/sanitized for specific vulnerability types.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_SOURCE_MAPPING_ENABLED
configurations.description: IAST: enables source mapping via SMAP/stratum information so IAST can map bytecode locations (class + line) back to original source file and line (for example for JSP-generated classes). When disabled, the source mapper is not available.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_SOURCE_MAPPING_MAX_SIZE
configurations.description: IAST: maximum number of classes for which SMAP/stratum source mappings are stored. Once this limit is reached, the tracer stops analyzing additional classes for source mapping to cap memory usage. Default: 1000.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_STACKTRACE_ENABLED
configurations.description: IAST (deprecated): alias for `DD_IAST_STACK_TRACE_ENABLED`. When enabled, the tracer captures a user-code stack trace and attaches it to IAST vulnerability reports (via a stack id).

configurations.name: DD_IAST_STACKTRACE_LEAK_SUPPRESS
configurations.description: IAST (deprecated): alias for `DD_IAST_STACK_TRACE_LEAK_SUPPRESS`. When enabled and IAST is fully enabled, suppresses stack trace leaks in Tomcat error pages by replacing the error report with a safe template instead of rendering the original stack trace. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_STACK_TRACE_ENABLED
configurations.description: IAST: when enabled (default), captures a user-code stack trace and attaches it to IAST vulnerability reports (via a stack id). Useful for locating where vulnerable code executed. This also accepts the deprecated alias `DD_IAST_STACKTRACE_ENABLED`.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_STACK_TRACE_LEAK_SUPPRESS
configurations.description: IAST: when enabled and IAST is fully enabled, suppresses stack trace leaks in Tomcat error pages by replacing the error report with a safe template instead of rendering the original stack trace. This also accepts the deprecated alias `DD_IAST_STACKTRACE_LEAK_SUPPRESS`. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_TELEMETRY_VERBOSITY
configurations.description: IAST: controls IAST telemetry verbosity (`OFF`, `MANDATORY`, `INFORMATION`, `DEBUG`). When tracer telemetry is disabled, this is forced to `OFF`. Default: `INFORMATION`.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_TRUNCATION_MAX_VALUE_LENGTH
configurations.description: Default value is 250

configurations.name: DD_IAST_VULNERABILITIES_PER_REQUEST
configurations.description: IAST: maximum number of vulnerabilities that can be reported per request (quota/overhead control). In `FULL` detection mode this limit is removed; in `DEFAULT` detection mode the default is 2.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_WEAK_CIPHER_ALGORITHMS
configurations.description: IAST: regular expression (matched against the uppercased cipher algorithm id) that defines which cipher algorithms are considered weak. When a used cipher algorithm matches this pattern, IAST reports a `WEAK_CIPHER` vulnerability.

configurations.name: DD_IAST_WEAK_HASH_ALGORITHMS
configurations.description: IAST: comma-separated set of hash algorithm ids considered weak (compared against the uppercased algorithm id). When a used hashing algorithm is in this set, IAST reports a `WEAK_HASH` vulnerability (for example for `MD5` or `SHA1`).

configurations.name: DD_ID_GENERATION_STRATEGY
configurations.description: Tracer: selects the trace/span id generation strategy (for example `RANDOM`, `SECURE_RANDOM`, `SEQUENTIAL`). Unknown values fall back to `RANDOM`, and non-random strategies emit a warning because they can impact trace correctness. Default: `RANDOM`.

configurations.name: DD_IGNITE_CACHE_INCLUDE_KEYS
configurations.description: Ignite: when enabled, includes the cache key as a span tag (`ignite.cache.key`) on Ignite cache spans. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_INJECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Single Step Instrumentation (SSI): marker/metadata indicating the tracer was injected by SSI (and potentially which components were injected). When present, the bootstrap sets the instrumentation source to `ssi`, and other subsystems may use the value (for example, profiling treats it as injected when it contains `profiler`).

configurations.name: DD_INJECT_FORCE
configurations.description: Single Step Instrumentation (SSI): forces the tracer javaagent to load even when multiple JVM agents are detected. This bypasses SSI guardrails that would otherwise abort startup to avoid running multiple agents. Can be set via `DD_INJECT_FORCE` or system property `dd.inject.force`. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_CONFIG_ID
configurations.description: Telemetry metadata: instrumentation configuration id. When set, it is propagated in the tracer telemetry as a configuration entry named `instrumentation_config_id`, allowing correlation to the configuration/injection that produced this tracer setup. Default: empty string.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_INSTALL_ID
configurations.description: Sets the install ID included in instrumentation telemetry payloads and headers. This can be used to correlate telemetry to a specific installation.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_INSTALL_TIME
configurations.description: Sets the install time included in instrumentation telemetry payloads and headers.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_INSTALL_TYPE
configurations.description: Sets the install type included in instrumentation telemetry payloads and headers.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_SOURCE
configurations.description: Telemetry/metadata: indicates how the tracer was installed (for example `manual`, `ssi`, `cmd_line`). The bootstrap may set this automatically for injected installs. Default: `manual`.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_TELEMETRY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Datadog may collect environmental and diagnostic information about your system to improve the product. When false, this telemetry data will not be collected. **Default**: true

configurations.name: DD_INTEGRATIONS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Tracer: global toggle for automatic instrumentation integrations. When disabled, integrations are not enabled by default (effectively disabling auto-instrumentation unless explicitly enabled). Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_INTEGRATION_JUNIT_ENABLED
configurations.description: CI Visibility (legacy): backward-compatible toggle that enables CI Visibility when set to `true` (legacy `dd.integration.junit.enabled` / `DD_INTEGRATION_JUNIT_ENABLED`). Prefer using `DD_CIVISIBILITY_ENABLED` instead.

configurations.name: DD_INTEGRATION_SYNAPSE_LEGACY_OPERATION_NAME
configurations.description: Synapse integration: when enabled, uses the legacy server span operation name `http.request` instead of the newer Synapse-specific operation name. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_INTEGRATION_TESTNG_ENABLED
configurations.description: CI Visibility (legacy): backward-compatible toggle that enables CI Visibility when set to `true` (legacy `dd.integration.testng.enabled` / `DD_INTEGRATION_TESTNG_ENABLED`). Prefer using `DD_CIVISIBILITY_ENABLED` instead.

configurations.name: DD_INTERNAL_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_ONLY_LOCAL_ROOT
configurations.description: Exception Replay: when enabled, captures/analyzes exceptions only on local-root spans (not intermediate spans) when exception replay is active. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_INTERNAL_FORCE_SYMBOL_DATABASE_UPLOAD
configurations.description: Live Debugging Symbol Database: forces symbol extraction/upload to start immediately at startup instead of waiting for remote configuration to request it. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_JDK_SOCKET_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enable native JDK support for Unix Domain Sockets.

configurations.name: DD_JMS_PROPAGATION_DISABLED_QUEUES
configurations.description: JMS: comma-separated list of queue names for which trace-context propagation is disabled (no header injection/extraction). Useful for mixed environments or when propagation should be avoided for specific destinations.

configurations.name: DD_JMS_PROPAGATION_DISABLED_TOPICS
configurations.description: JMS: comma-separated list of topic names for which trace-context propagation is disabled (no header injection/extraction). Useful for mixed environments or when propagation should be avoided for specific destinations.

configurations.name: DD_JMS_PROPAGATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: null

configurations.name: DD_JMS_UNACKNOWLEDGED_MAX_AGE
configurations.description: JMS: maximum age (in seconds) of the oldest unacknowledged message span in client-acknowledge sessions before the tracer implicitly finishes the captured spans, preventing unbounded accumulation. Default: 3600.

configurations.name: DD_JMXFETCH_CHECK_PERIOD
configurations.description: How often to send JMX metrics (in ms).

configurations.name: DD_JMXFETCH_CONFIG
configurations.description: Additional metrics configuration file for JMX metrics collection. The Java Agent looks for `jvm_direct:true` in the `instance` section in the `yaml` file to change configuration.

configurations.name: DD_JMXFETCH_CONFIG_DIR
configurations.description: Additional configuration directory for JMX metrics collection. The Java Agent looks for `jvm_direct:true` in the `instance` section in the `yaml` file to change configuration.

configurations.name: DD_JMXFETCH_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enable collection of JMX metrics by Java Tracing Agent.

configurations.name: DD_JMXFETCH_INITIAL_REFRESH_BEANS_PERIOD
configurations.description: JMXFetch: sets the initial beans refresh period passed to JMXFetch (controls how often it refreshes the list of MBeans during the initial phase). If unset, JMXFetch uses its default behavior.

configurations.name: DD_JMXFETCH_METRICS_CONFIGS
configurations.description: JMXFetch (deprecated): comma-separated list of additional metric config YAML files to load (passed to JMXFetch as metric config files).

configurations.name: DD_JMXFETCH_MULTIPLE_RUNTIME_SERVICES_ENABLED
configurations.description: JMXFetch/runtime metrics: when enabled, collects service names from entry traces and supplies them to JMXFetch so runtime metrics can be reported for multiple services (up to the configured limit). Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_JMXFETCH_MULTIPLE_RUNTIME_SERVICES_LIMIT
configurations.description: JMXFetch/runtime metrics: maximum number of distinct service names to track/report when multiple-runtime-services is enabled. Default: 10.

configurations.name: DD_JMXFETCH_REFRESH_BEANS_PERIOD
configurations.description: How often to refresh list of available JMX beans (in seconds).

configurations.name: DD_JMXFETCH_START_DELAY
configurations.description: JMXFetch/JMX initialization: delay (in seconds) before starting the JMX subsystem (used by JMXFetch and other features that require JMX). Default: 15.

configurations.name: DD_JMXFETCH_STATSD_HOST
configurations.description: Override the address of the trace Agent host that the default tracer attempts to submit DogStatsD metrics to. Use `DD_AGENT_HOST` to override `DD_DOGSTATSD_HOST`.

configurations.name: DD_JMXFETCH_STATSD_PORT
configurations.description: Overrides the default trace Agent port for DogStatsD metric submission. If the Agent configuration sets `dogstatsd_port` or `DD_DOGSTATSD_PORT` to something other than the default `8125`, then the library configuration `DD_DOGSTATSD_PORT` must match it.

configurations.name: DD_KAFKA_CLIENT_BASE64_DECODING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Kafka client propagation: when enabled, base64-decodes Kafka header values before extracting trace context (helps when header values are base64-mangled). Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_KAFKA_CLIENT_PROPAGATION_DISABLED_TOPICS
configurations.description: Kafka client propagation: comma-separated list of topic names for which trace-context propagation is disabled (no header injection/extraction). Useful for mixed client environments or to avoid propagation for specific topics.

configurations.name: DD_KAFKA_CLIENT_PROPAGATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: null

configurations.name: DD_KAFKA_PROPAGATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: null

configurations.name: DD_LLMOBS_AGENTLESS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Optional - _integer or string_ - **default**: `false` Only required if you are not using the Datadog Agent, in which case this should be set to `1` or `true`.

configurations.name: DD_LLMOBS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Toggle to enable submitting data to LLM Observability

configurations.name: DD_LLMOBS_ML_APP
configurations.description: Sets the default ML app name used for LLM Observability data. This value is required when LLM Observability is enabled.

configurations.name: DD_LOGS_INJECTION
configurations.description: **Supported Input**: Boolean (`true`/`false`) **Caveats**: Not supported in C++ or Go. **Description**: Enables or disables the automatic injection of trace context (trace ID, span ID) into JSON/structured application logs. This allows for correlation between traces and logs.

configurations.name: DD_LOGS_INJECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: **Supported Input**: Boolean (`true`/`false`) **Caveats**: Not supported in C++ or Go. **Description**: Enables or disables the automatic injection of trace context (trace ID, span ID) into JSON/structured application logs. This allows for correlation between traces and logs.

configurations.name: DD_LOG_FORMAT_JSON
configurations.description: Set to 'true' to output logs in JSON format

configurations.name: DD_LOG_LEVEL
configurations.description: Sets the Datadog Agent logging level. Valid values are trace, debug, info, warn, error, critical, or off.

configurations.name: DD_MEASURE_METHODS
configurations.description: Tracing: list of method patterns to mark spans as `measured`. Spans created from matching methods will have `measured=true` which affects how they are included in tracer metrics/stats. Format is the same as `DD_TRACE_METHODS`. Default: empty.

configurations.name: DD_MESSAGE_BROKER_SPLIT_BY_DESTINATION
configurations.description: Messaging: when enabled, uses the destination (topic/queue) as the service name for message broker spans (for example time-in-queue spans), effectively splitting services by destination. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_METRICS_OTEL_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables OpenTelemetry metrics export. Metrics are disabled by default and are only enabled when this is set to a truthy value.

configurations.name: DD_METRICS_OTEL_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Internal equivalent of OTEL_METRIC_EXPORT_INTERVAL

configurations.name: DD_METRICS_OTEL_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Internal equivalent of OTEL_METRIC_EXPORT_TIMEOUT

configurations.name: DD_OBFUSCATION_QUERY_STRING_REGEXP
configurations.description: HTTP query string obfuscation (legacy alias for `DD_TRACE_OBFUSCATION_QUERY_STRING_REGEXP`): regular expression used to redact sensitive values in the `http.query` tag (and the query portion of `http.url`). `null` uses a built-in default pattern; an empty string disables query obfuscation. Matches are replaced with `<redacted>`.

configurations.name: DD_OPTIMIZED_MAP_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the optimized `TagMap` implementation used for storing span tags. When disabled, the tracer uses the legacy tag-map implementation. Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_OTLP_METRICS_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: Internal equivalent of OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_ENDPOINT

configurations.name: DD_OTLP_METRICS_HEADERS
configurations.description: OTLP metrics export: additional headers to send with OTLP metrics requests, encoded as comma-separated `key=value` pairs (for example `api-key=...`). Parsed into a map and combined with `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_HEADERS` when OpenTelemetry metrics is enabled. Default: empty.

configurations.name: DD_OTLP_METRICS_PROTOCOL
configurations.description: Internal equivalent of OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_PROTOCOL

configurations.name: DD_OTLP_METRICS_TEMPORALITY_PREFERENCE
configurations.description: Internal equivalent of OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_TEMPORALITY_PREFERENCE

configurations.name: DD_OTLP_METRICS_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Internal equivalent of OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_TIMEOUT

configurations.name: DD_PIPELINE_EXECUTION_ID
configurations.description: Sets the pipeline execution ID reported as CI metadata for AWS CodePipeline builds. This value is used as the CI pipeline identifier when running a CodeBuild job initiated by CodePipeline.

configurations.name: DD_PRIMARY_TAG
configurations.description: Primary tag value used in base-hash calculation and included in Data Streams Monitoring payloads (field `PrimaryTag`). Changing it affects the computed base hash and DSM aggregation. Often used as an extra global dimension (for example, a region).

configurations.name: DD_PRIORITIZATION_TYPE
configurations.description: Trace writer prioritization strategy (`FastLane` / `EnsureTrace`). `FastLane` (default) drops traces under backpressure; `EnsureTrace` blocks to ensure "kept" traces are enqueued (not recommended for production).

configurations.name: DD_PRIORITY_SAMPLING
configurations.description: **INI**: `datadog.priority_sampling` Whether to enable priority sampling.

configurations.name: DD_PRIORITY_SAMPLING_FORCE
configurations.description: Trace sampling: forces the priority sampling decision when priority sampling is enabled. Set to `KEEP` to force `SAMPLER_KEEP`, or `DROP` to force `SAMPLER_DROP`; otherwise the tracer uses normal rate-by-service sampling. Default: unset.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_AGENTLESS
configurations.description: Enables agentless profile upload, sending profiles directly to the profiling intake instead of through a local agent.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ALLOCATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: If set to `true`, enables Allocation profiling (in Preview). Defaults to `false`.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_APIKEY
configurations.description: Deprecated: legacy profiling API key environment variable (`DD_PROFILING_APIKEY`). Used as a fallback to populate the Datadog API key when `DD_API_KEY` / `DD_API_KEY_FILE` are not set.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_APIKEY_FILE
configurations.description: Deprecated: legacy profiling API key file path (`DD_PROFILING_APIKEY_FILE`). If set, the tracer reads the API key from this file as a fallback when `DD_API_KEY` / `DD_API_KEY_FILE` are not set.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_API_KEY
configurations.description: Deprecated: legacy profiling API key environment variable (`DD_PROFILING_API_KEY`). Used as a fallback to populate the Datadog API key when `DD_API_KEY` / `DD_API_KEY_FILE` are not set.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_API_KEY_FILE
configurations.description: Deprecated: legacy profiling API key file path (`DD_PROFILING_API_KEY_FILE`). If set, the tracer reads the API key from this file as a fallback when `DD_API_KEY` / `DD_API_KEY_FILE` are not set.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ASYNC_ALLOC_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Datadog native profiler (legacy `async` key): allocation/memory sampling interval in bytes used for allocation and live-heap profiling (`memory=<interval>b`). This is a legacy alias of `DD_PROFILING_DDPROF_ALLOC_INTERVAL`. Default: 262144 (256KB).

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ASYNC_CPU_ENABLED
configurations.description: Datadog native profiler (legacy `async` key): enables/disables CPU profiling. This is a legacy alias of `DD_PROFILING_DDPROF_CPU_ENABLED`. Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ASYNC_CPU_INTERVAL_MS
configurations.description: Datadog native profiler (legacy `async` key): CPU sampling interval in milliseconds (used as `cpu=<interval>m`). This is a legacy alias of `DD_PROFILING_DDPROF_CPU_INTERVAL_MS`. Default: 10ms.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ASYNC_CSTACK
configurations.description: Datadog native profiler (legacy `async` key): configures how native stack traces are collected (for example `vm`, `dwarf`, `fp`, `no`), passed as `cstack=<mode>`. On non-HotSpot VMs, `vm*` modes fall back to `dwarf`. This is a legacy alias of `DD_PROFILING_DDPROF_CSTACK`. Default: `vm`.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ASYNC_DEBUG_LIB
configurations.description: Datadog native profiler (legacy `async` key): path to the Datadog profiler native library debug build. This is a legacy alias of `DD_PROFILING_DDPROF_DEBUG_LIB`.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ASYNC_LINENUMBERS
configurations.description: Datadog native profiler (legacy `async` key): controls whether line numbers are included in stack traces. When disabled, the profiler omits line numbers (`linenumbers=f`). This is a legacy alias of `DD_PROFILING_DDPROF_LINENUMBERS`. Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ASYNC_LIVEHEAP_CAPACITY
configurations.description: Datadog native profiler (legacy `async` key): live-heap (memory leak) tracking capacity (maximum number of entries tracked), clamped to an upper bound (8192). This is a legacy alias of `DD_PROFILING_DDPROF_LIVEHEAP_CAPACITY`. Default: 1024.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ASYNC_LIVEHEAP_ENABLED
configurations.description: Datadog native profiler (legacy `async` key): enables live-heap (memory leak) profiling. This is a legacy alias of `DD_PROFILING_DDPROF_LIVEHEAP_ENABLED`. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ASYNC_LIVEHEAP_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Datadog native profiler (legacy `async` key): live-heap (memory leak) profiling interval parameter. This is a legacy alias of `DD_PROFILING_DDPROF_LIVEHEAP_INTERVAL` and controls the underlying live-heap/memleak tracking interval used by the native profiler. Default is computed from max heap size and live-heap capacity (roughly `maxHeap / capacity`; falls back to 1048576 when max heap is unknown).

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ASYNC_LIVEHEAP_SAMPLE_PERCENT
configurations.description: Datadog native profiler (legacy `async` key): live-heap (memory leak) profiling sample percentage. This is a legacy alias of `DD_PROFILING_DDPROF_LIVEHEAP_SAMPLE_PERCENT` and is used to compute the sampling fraction passed to the profiler (`<percent>/100`). Default: 50.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ASYNC_LOGLEVEL
