configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_5_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Jakarta Servlet 5 instrumentation for tracing correlation and security (supports RUM request/response wrapping and async commits, enriches active servlet spans with user principal, and enables IAST taint tracking for request/response/multipart plus session URL-rewriting detection)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Servlet integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Servlet integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_ASYNC_TIMEOUT_ERROR
configurations.description: By default, long running asynchronous requests will be marked as an error, setting this value to false allows to mark all timeouts as successful requests.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_COOKIE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Servlet Cookie (Cookie) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_DISPATCHER_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Servlet dispatcher integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_DISPATCHER_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Servlet dispatcher integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_DISPATCHER_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Servlet `RequestDispatcher` tracing (creates spans for `forward`/`include`/`error`, sets the resource name to the dispatch target, and injects trace context into the request to preserve propagation across dispatches)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Servlet API instrumentation (creates inbound HTTP request spans for Servlet 2.x/3.x and Jakarta Servlet 5, including async dispatch support and AppSec request blocking; when IAST is enabled, taints request inputs (params/headers/cookies/body/multipart) and monitors session usage)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_FILTER_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Filter integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_FILTER_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Filter integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_FILTER_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Servlet Filter (Filter) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_PRINCIPAL_ENABLED
configurations.description: When `true`, user principal is collected. Available for versions 0.61+.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_REQUEST_BODY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Servlet AppSec request-body collection (wraps `HttpServletRequest.getInputStream/getReader` for Servlet 2.x/3.0, Servlet 3.1+, and Jakarta Servlet 5 to capture body bytes/chars and feed AppSec callbacks for WAF inspection/blocking)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_REQUEST_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Servlet request IAST instrumentation (taints request headers/params/cookies/query/body, tracks unvalidated redirects via `getRequestDispatcher`, and reports session URL-rewriting risks via `getSession` when IAST is enabled)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_RESPONSE_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Servlet response integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_RESPONSE_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Servlet response integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_RESPONSE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Servlet response instrumentation (creates `servlet.response` spans for `sendError`/`sendRedirect` and, when IAST is enabled, tracks response headers/cookies plus redirect/URL-encoding sinks for vulnerability detection)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_ROOT_CONTEXT_SERVICE_NAME
configurations.description: Servlet root context service name: when servlet-context based service naming is applied and the servlet context is `/`, this value is used as the service name instead of an empty name. Default: `root-servlet`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_SERVICE_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the HttpServlet integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_SERVICE_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the HttpServlet integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_SERVICE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Servlet Service (HttpServlet) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SERVLET_SESSION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Servlet Session (HttpSession) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SETUP_TEARDOWN_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables CI Visibility setup/teardown tracing for JUnit 4 and JUnit 5 (creates spans for `@Before/@After` and `@BeforeEach/@AfterEach` operations via internal runners/extensions)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SFN_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables SFN (SfnClient) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SFN_INJECT_DATADOG_ATTRIBUTE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether or not the "_datadog" attribute, which holds trace context, is injected into SFN inputs

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SHUTDOWN_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Shutdown instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SLICK_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Slick (SlickRunnable) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SNAKEYAML_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables SnakeYAML instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SNS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables SNS (SnsClient) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SNS_INJECT_DATADOG_ATTRIBUTE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether or not the "_datadog" attribute, which holds trace context, is injected into SNS requests

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SOCKET_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Socket (SocketConnect) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPAN_ATTRIBUTE_SCHEMA
configurations.description: Sets the schema version for service naming and span attributes. Accepted values are: "v1", "v0". Default value is "v0".

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPAN_TAGS
configurations.description: A list of default tags to be added to every span.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPARKJAVA_2_4_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables SparkJava 2.4 (Routes) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPARKJAVA_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables SparkJava (Routes) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPARK_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Apache Spark tracing for data jobs (injects the Datadog Spark listener into `SparkContext`/`LiveListenerBus`, captures application lifecycle for job execution, and can enrich Spark SQL plans / integrate with OpenLineage when enabled)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPARK_EXECUTOR_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the SparkExecutor integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPARK_EXECUTOR_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the SparkExecutor integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPARK_EXECUTOR_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spark Executor (SparkExecutor) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPARK_EXIT_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spark Exit (SparkExit) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPARK_OPENLINEAGE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spark OpenLineage (OpenLineage) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPLIT_BY_TAGS
configurations.description: Used to rename the service name associated with spans to be identified with the corresponding span tag

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRAY_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the SprayHttpServer integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRAY_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the SprayHttpServer integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRAY_HTTP_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spray HTTP (SprayHttpServer) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRAY_HTTP_SERVER_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spray HTTP Server (SprayHttpServer) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_ASYNC_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring Async (SpringAsync) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_BEANS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring Beans (BeanFactory) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_BOOT_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring Boot startup instrumentation (sets the tracer service name from `spring.application.name` when not user-set, adds Spring Boot process tags, and detects WAR deployments via `SpringBootServletInitializer`)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_BOOT_SPAN_ORIGIN_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring Boot Span Origin (SBCodeOrigin) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_CLOUD_ZUUL_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring Cloud Zuul proxy instrumentation (prevents forwarded requests from overwriting Datadog propagation headers and updates the inbound span route/resource name with the downstream Spring controller pattern after forwarding)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_CORE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring Core (StreamUtils) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_DATA_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the SpringRepository integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_DATA_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the SpringRepository integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_DATA_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring Data (SpringRepository) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_JMS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring JMS (AbstractPollingMessageListenerContainer) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_MESSAGING_4_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring Messaging 4 (SpringMessageHandler) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_MESSAGING_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the SpringMessageHandler integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_MESSAGING_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the SpringMessageHandler integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_MESSAGING_E2E_DURATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Spring Messaging end-to-end duration: when enabled for `spring-messaging`, the messaging decorator calls `span.beginEndToEnd()` at span start, enabling end-to-end duration calculation when spans are finished with end-to-end semantics. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_MESSAGING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring Messaging (SpringMessageHandler) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_PATH_FILTER_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring path/filter helpers for improved route naming (registers `ServletRequestPathFilter` and Datadog's `HandlerMappingResourceNameFilter` early in the filter chain to set best-matching patterns and servlet path handling)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_RABBIT_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring AMQP (spring-rabbit) consumer tracing (propagates context from `Delivery` to listener execution and creates measured `amqp.consume` spans tagged with the consumer queue)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_SCHEDULING_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the SpringScheduling integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_SCHEDULING_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the SpringScheduling integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_SCHEDULING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring Scheduling (SpringScheduling) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_SCHEDULING_LEGACY_TRACING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Spring Scheduling legacy tracing: when enabled, scheduled task spans are started with an implicit parent (linked to the currently active span). When disabled, scheduled task spans are started with an explicit `null` parent (new trace). Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_SECURITY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring Security AppSec user-event instrumentation (records login/signup/user context events from AuthenticationManager/SecurityContextHolder/UserDetailsManager and signals user-not-found events when AppSec is active)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_WEBFLUX_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Spring WebFlux integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_WEBFLUX_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Spring WebFlux integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_WEBFLUX_CLIENT_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the WebClientFilter integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_WEBFLUX_CLIENT_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the WebClientFilter integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_WEBFLUX_CLIENT_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring WebFlux Client (WebClientFilter) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_WEBFLUX_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring WebFlux tracing and security (creates server spans for `DispatcherHandler`/handler execution and finishes them on reactive completion, updates route resource names from best-matching patterns, adds WebClient outbound tracing, and enables IAST taint tracking for reactive request/response data)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_WEBFLUX_FUNCTIONAL_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring WebFlux Functional (RouterFunction) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_WEB_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Spring Web integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_WEB_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Spring Web integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_WEB_CODE_ORIGIN_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring Web Code Origin (SpringWebCodeOrigin) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_WEB_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring Web MVC instrumentation (creates handler/controller spans and view-render spans, sets resource names from HandlerMapping best-matching patterns, supports async CompletionStage continuations, and adds AppSec/IAST hooks for endpoint collection and path/template/matrix parameter processing/blocking)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_WS_2_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring WS 2 (MethodEndpoint) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPRING_WS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Spring WS (MethodEndpoint) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPYMEMCACHED_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Spymemcached integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPYMEMCACHED_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Spymemcached integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPYMEMCACHED_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables spymemcached client tracing (creates spans for `MemcachedClient` operations including async futures/bulk gets and `incr`/`decr`, finishes spans via completion listeners, and tags peer connection addresses)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SQS_BODY_PROPAGATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: SQS body propagation: when enabled, if the `_datadog` SQS message attribute is not present, the tracer attempts to parse the message body as JSON and extract `MessageAttributes._datadog` for context propagation. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SQS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables AWS SQS instrumentation (requests `AWSTraceHeader` for receives, wraps received message lists to extract/propagate trace context, and adds AWS SDK v2 SQS interceptors / JMS helpers when propagation or Data Streams is enabled)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SQS_INJECT_DATADOG_ATTRIBUTE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether or not the "_datadog" attribute, is injected into SQS messageAttributes

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SQS_LEGACY_TRACING_ENABLED
configurations.description: SQS legacy tracing: when enabled (and inferred services are allowed), uses legacy tracing/naming behavior for SQS spans; this also changes the default enablement of SQS time-in-queue tracking (disabled by default in legacy mode). Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SQS_PROPAGATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: SQS context propagation: when enabled, extracts incoming distributed context from SQS messages (message attributes and/or body, depending on configuration) and uses it as the parent context for consumer spans; also ensures required SQS attributes (e.g. `AWSTraceHeader`) are requested. Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SQS_TIME_IN_QUEUE_ENABLED
configurations.description: SQS time-in-queue: when enabled (and inferred services are allowed), creates a time-in-queue span starting at the message `SentTimestamp` and parents the consumer span to it, representing time spent in SQS before delivery. Default: enabled for non-legacy SQS traces.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SSLSOCKET_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables SSLSocket instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_STARTUP_LOGS
configurations.description: Enable startup configuration and diagnostic log.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_STATS_COMPUTATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether client-side trace statistics computation is enabled. Defaults to true.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_STATUS404DECORATOR_ENABLED
configurations.description: Tag interceptor rule `Status404Decorator`: when enabled (together with `URLAsResourceNameRule` and `Status404Rule`), sets the span resource name to `404` when the HTTP status code is 404. Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_STATUS404RULE_ENABLED
configurations.description: By default, HTTP 404 responses use "404" as the span resource name. When `false`, HTTP 404 responses keep the original URL path as the resource name.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_STRICT_WRITES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Strict trace writes: when enabled, enforces strict pending-reference accounting when deciding to write a trace (throws if the pending reference count becomes negative, and writes as soon as the reference count reaches zero). Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_STRUCTURED_TASK_SCOPE_21_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Structured Task Scope (JDK 21) (StructuredTaskScope21) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_STRUCTURED_TASK_SCOPE_25_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Structured Task Scope (JDK 25) (StructuredTaskScope25) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_STRUCTURED_TASK_SCOPE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables context propagation for Java structured concurrency (`StructuredTaskScope`) subtasks (captures the active span at Subtask creation and restores it when the Runnable executes; supports JDK 21–24 and JDK 25+ implementations)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SUREFIRE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Surefire (JUnit4ClassOrder) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SYNAPSE3_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Synapse 3 integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SYNAPSE3_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Synapse 3 integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SYNAPSE3_CLIENT_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Apache Synapse 3 client-side tracing (creates spans for outgoing requests via `TargetHandler`, injects distributed trace headers into target requests, propagates parent spans through passthru/ClientWorker continuations, and finishes spans on final responses or errors)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SYNAPSE3_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Apache Synapse 3 pass-through transport tracing (creates client and server spans for `TargetHandler`/`SourceHandler` HTTP processing, injects/extracts distributed trace headers, propagates context across worker threads/continuations, and strips `x-datadog*` headers on passthru to avoid leakage)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SYNAPSE3_SERVER_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Apache Synapse 3 server-side tracing (creates spans for incoming requests via `SourceHandler`, extracts distributed trace headers, propagates context through `ServerWorker`, and finishes spans on final responses or errors)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TAGS
configurations.description: A list of default tags to be added to every span, profile, and JMX metric. If DD_ENV or DD_VERSION is used, it overrides any env or version tag defined in DD_TAGS. Available for versions 0.50.0+.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TASK_RUNNER_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Task Runner (TaskRunner) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TASK_UNWRAPPING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Task Unwrapping (TaskUnwrapping) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TESTNG_6_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables TestNG 6 (TestNGClassListener) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TESTNG_7_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables TestNG 7 (TestNGClassListener) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TESTNG_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables CI Visibility instrumentation for TestNG (installs Datadog listeners to report suite/class/test spans, and supports ITR test skipping, fail-fast test ordering, and automatic test retries when execution policies are enabled)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TESTNG_ITR_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables TestNG ITR (TestNGSkip) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TEST_ORDER_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables CI Visibility test ordering (applies Datadog fail-fast ordering for JUnit 4/5 and TestNG so tests are executed in a Datadog-provided priority order)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TEST_RETRY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables CI Visibility execution policies for tests (automatic test retries and failure-suppression across supported frameworks such as JUnit, TestNG, Cucumber, Karate, ScalaTest, and MUnit)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_THREAD_POOL_EXECUTORS_EXCLUDE
configurations.description: ThreadPoolExecutor instrumentation exclude list: list of fully-qualified `ThreadPoolExecutor` implementation class names that should not be instrumented for task context propagation/wrapping. Default: unset.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_THREAD_POOL_EXECUTORS_LEGACY_TRACING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Thread pool executors legacy tracing: when enabled, the thread-pool-executors instrumentation uses wrapping-based propagation for tasks (wrapping `Runnable`s). When disabled (default), it uses field-backed context storage on tasks (and a ThreadLocal between executor hooks) instead of wrapping.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_THROWABLES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables exception profiling (`throwables`) when JFR is available (records `Exception`/`Error` instantiation events for continuous profiling and excludes known control-flow/leak-detection exceptions like Hikari's `ProxyLeakTask`)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_THYMELEAF_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Thymeleaf IAST XSS instrumentation (tracks unescaped template output via `StandardUtextTagProcessor` and `ElementTagStructureHandler.setBody`, and reports XSS sinks with template name and line)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TIBCO_BW_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Tibco BusinessWorks integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TIBCO_BW_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Tibco BusinessWorks integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TIBCO_BW_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables TIBCO BusinessWorks (BW) tracing (creates spans for process instances and activities/tasks, propagates parent/child process context, and can derive the service name from the BW application/module)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TIBCO_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables TIBCO BusinessWorks tracing including thread-pool context propagation (creates spans for BW processes and activities, and wraps scheduled runnables to keep trace continuity across worker threads)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TINYLOG_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tinylog 2 log correlation (injects trace/span IDs plus `dd.service`, `dd.env`, and `dd.version` into Tinylog's log context when logs injection is enabled)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TOMCAT_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Tomcat integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TOMCAT_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Tomcat integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TOMCAT_CLASSLOADING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Tomcat Classloading (WebappClassLoader) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TOMCAT_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Apache Tomcat server tracing and security instrumentation (creates inbound HTTP request spans via `CoyoteAdapter`, finishes them on request/response recycle, supports AppSec request blocking and request-body parameter collection, tags Tomcat process info, and propagates context through Tomcat WebSocket handshakes/upgrades)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TOMCAT_WEBSOCKET_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Tomcat WebSocket context propagation (captures the active HTTP handshake span in `WsHandshakeRequest` and re-activates it for `WsHttpUpgradeHandler` initialization)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TRACER_METRICS_BUFFERING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Tracer metrics buffering: when enabled, tracer metrics payloads are buffered and sent asynchronously when agent request latency is high; when disabled, metrics are sent synchronously (no buffering). Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TRACER_METRICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Tracer metrics: enables computation and reporting of tracer metrics (client stats) to the Datadog Agent. Note: metrics are only enabled when APM tracing is enabled (`isTracerMetricsEnabled()` also checks `isApmTracingEnabled()`). Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TRACER_METRICS_IGNORED_RESOURCES
configurations.description: Tracer metrics ignored resources: list of span resource names for which tracer metrics should not be computed. If a span's resource name matches an ignored entry, the tracer skips metrics publication for that trace (and its children). Default: unset.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TRACER_METRICS_MAX_AGGREGATES
configurations.description: Tracer metrics max aggregates: limits the number of distinct metric aggregates kept by the tracer metrics aggregator (conflation pool/pending map sizing). Default: 2048.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TRACER_METRICS_MAX_PENDING
configurations.description: Tracer metrics max pending: size of the internal pending queue used by the tracer metrics aggregator for incoming metric events; when the queue is full, new metric events may be dropped. Default: 2048.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TRACE_ANNOTATION_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the TraceAnnotations integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TRACE_ANNOTATION_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the TraceAnnotations integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TRACE_ANNOTATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Trace Annotation (TraceAnnotations) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TRACE_CONFIG_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the TraceConfig integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TRACE_CONFIG_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the TraceConfig integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TRACE_CONFIG_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Trace Config (TraceConfig) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TRACE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables method tracing via trace annotations and configured trace methods (creates spans for methods annotated with `datadog.trace.api.Trace` and other supported `@Trace` annotations, and for class/methods listed in tracer configuration)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TRIAGE
configurations.description: Triage mode: when enabled, turns on additional diagnostics/metrics intended for troubleshooting (for example instrumenter matching/transform timing), and causes tracer flares to include thread dumps even when debug logging is not enabled. Note: setting `DD_TRIAGE_REPORT_TRIGGER` implicitly enables triage mode unless overridden.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TWILIO_SDK_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Twilio SDK integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TWILIO_SDK_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Twilio SDK integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_TWILIO_SDK_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Twilio Java SDK client tracing (creates spans for Twilio `create/read/fetch/update/delete` operations, including async calls finished via callbacks, and tags spans with operation/result details)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_UNDERTOW_2_0_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Undertow 2.0 server tracing and AppSec hooks (creates inbound HTTP spans, propagates context across `HttpServerExchange.dispatch`, bridges to `javax.servlet` requests, and enables response/header callbacks plus form/multipart body inspection and blocking)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_UNDERTOW_2_2_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Undertow 2.2 servlet/request parsing instrumentation (propagates tracing context into `jakarta.servlet` requests and records a 400 span when HTTP request parsing fails before a normal server span can be created)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_UNDERTOW_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Undertow server tracing and security instrumentation (creates inbound HTTP spans around `Connectors.executeRootHandler`, propagates context across `HttpServerExchange.dispatch`, bridges to servlet requests (javax/jakarta), records 400 spans on request-parse failures, and supports AppSec response/header callbacks plus form/multipart body inspection and blocking)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_UNDERTOW_HTTP_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Undertow HTTP integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_UNDERTOW_HTTP_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Undertow HTTP integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_UNDERTOW_HTTP_SERVER_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Undertow HTTP server integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_UNDERTOW_HTTP_SERVER_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Undertow HTTP server integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_UNDERTOW_LEGACY_TRACING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Undertow legacy tracing: when enabled, Undertow HTTP server instrumentation uses legacy tracing behavior (including how it sets framework route/resource names during servlet dispatch). Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_UNDERTOW_REQUEST_PARSE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Undertow Request Parse (HttpRequestParser) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_URLASRESOURCENAMERULE_ENABLED
configurations.description: HTTP URL-as-resource-name rule (`URLAsResourceNameRule`): when enabled, HTTP server spans can use the request URL/path to compute the span resource name (and participates in 404 resource-name behavior). When disabled, the default resource name is `/` unless a framework route is set. Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_URLCONNECTION_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Url integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_URLCONNECTION_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Url integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_URLCONNECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables URLConnection (Url) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VALKEY_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: This setting is currently not used by the valkey-go integration and has no effect.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VALKEY_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Valkey integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VALKEY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Valkey instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VELOCITY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Velocity (ASTReference) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VERTX_3_4_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Vert.x Web 3.4 tracing and security hooks (creates spans for route handler execution, updates route-based resource names, and adds AppSec/IAST instrumentation for request bodies/JSON/session helpers, cookies, reroute redirects, and buffer taint propagation)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VERTX_3_5_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Vert.x 3.5 (VertxHttpHeaders) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VERTX_3_9_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Vert.x 3.9 (Vertx39HttpServertResponse) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VERTX_4_0_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Vert.x Web 4.0 tracing and security hooks (creates spans for route handler execution, updates route-based resource names, decorates HTTP client failures, and adds AppSec/IAST instrumentation for request/response JSON helpers, request-body processing, and buffer taint propagation)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VERTX_5_0_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Vert.x 5.0 (HttpServerRequest) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VERTX_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Vert.x integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VERTX_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Vert.x integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VERTX_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Vert.x instrumentation (creates spans for Vert.x Web route handlers and updates HTTP routes/resource names, traces Vert.x Redis and SQL clients, and adds AppSec/IAST hooks for request bodies, cookies, JSON helpers, and buffer taint propagation)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VERTX_REDIS_CLIENT_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Vert.x Redis client integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VERTX_REDIS_CLIENT_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Vert.x Redis client integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VERTX_REDIS_CLIENT_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Vert.x Redis client tracing (creates spans for Redis commands sent via `Redis.send`/`RedisAPI`, clones reused `Request` objects so each use gets its own span, wraps async handlers/futures to finish spans and preserve parent context, and tags spans with command + peer connection info)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VERTX_ROUTE_HANDLER_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Vert.x route handler integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VERTX_ROUTE_HANDLER_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Vert.x route handler integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VERTX_SQL_CLIENT_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the Vert.x SQL client integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VERTX_SQL_CLIENT_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Vert.x SQL client integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_VERTX_SQL_CLIENT_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Vert.x SQL client tracing (creates `database.query` spans for statement/prepared query execution and cursor reads, wraps async result handlers to finish spans and preserve parent context, and tags spans with SQL/operation plus DB connection metadata for Vert.x MySQL and PostgreSQL clients)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WALLCLOCK_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Wallclock (EnableWallclockProfiling) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WEAVER_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Weaver instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WEBSOCKET_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by the WebSocket integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WEBSOCKET_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the WebSocket integration

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WEBSOCKET_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables WebSocket tracing (links WebSocket sessions to the active HTTP handshake span, creates spans for WebSocket frame send/receive and session close events for javax/jakarta WebSocket APIs and Jetty WebSocket, and propagates handshake context through Tomcat WebSocket handshakes/upgrades)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing sent and received websocket messages (text and binary) and connection close events.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGES_INHERIT_SAMPLING
configurations.description: By default, websocket messages preserve the same sampling as the span captured during the handshake. This ensures that, if a handshake span has been sampled, all the messages in its session will also be sampled. To disable that behavior and sample each websocket message independently, set this configuration to `false`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGES_SEPARATE_TRACES
configurations.description: By default, each received message generates a new trace. The handshake is linked to it as a span link. Setting this parameter to `false` causes all the spans captured during the session to be in the same trace.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WEBSOCKET_TAG_SESSION_ID
configurations.description: When set to `true`, the websocket spans have the tag `websocket.session.id` containing the session ID when available.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WEBSPHERE_JMX_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables WebSphere JMX (WebsphereSecurity) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WILDFLY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables WildFly JEE env-entry classloader overrides (captures `EnvEntryInjectionSource` values and applies any JNDI entries under `java:comp/env/datadog/tags/*` as per-deployment tags so spans can be enriched via `ClassloaderConfigurationOverrides`)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_X_DATADOG_TAGS_MAX_LENGTH
configurations.description: Maximum length of the `x-datadog-tags` header for trace tag propagation. Set to 0 to disable.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ZIO_EXPERIMENTAL_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables ZIO Experimental (ZioRuntime) instrumentation

configurations.name: DD_TRIAGE_REPORT_DIR
configurations.description: Triage report directory: directory where the scheduled triage report (a tracer flare zip) is written when `DD_TRIAGE_REPORT_TRIGGER` is set. Default: `java.io.tmpdir`.

configurations.name: DD_TRIAGE_REPORT_TRIGGER
configurations.description: Triage report trigger: when set to a delay (parsed by `TimeUtils.parseSimpleDelay`, for example `3600s`), schedules generation of a triage report (tracer flare zip) after that delay. Setting this trigger also enables triage mode by default.

configurations.name: DD_UNSAFE_CLASS_INJECTION
configurations.description: Temporary internal flag in case we need to roll-back a new Java class-injection feature

configurations.name: DD_USM_ENABLED
configurations.description: Universal Service Monitoring (USM): when enabled, activates USM as a target system so USM-specific instrumentations/features can be installed by the Java tracer agent. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_VERSION
configurations.description: Set the application's version, for example: `1.2.3` or `6c44da20`.

configurations.name: DD_VISITOR_CLASS_PARSING
configurations.description: Temporary internal flag in case we need to roll-back a new Java class-parsing feature

configurations.name: DD_WRITER_BAGGAGE_INJECT
configurations.description: Baggage injection into outgoing span metadata: when enabled, includes baggage items as tags/metadata when serializing spans (combined with propagation tags); when disabled, only propagation tags are included. Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_WRITER_TYPE
configurations.description: Default value sends traces to the Agent. Configuring with `LoggingWriter` instead writes traces out to the console.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: Specifies the base URL for sending OTLP data for all signals unless overridden. **Default (gRPC)**: `http://localhost:4317`. **Default (HTTP)**: `http://localhost:4318`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies a comma-separated list of key-value pairs to be used as headers on all outgoing OTLP requests (for example, `api-key=key,other-config=value`).

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: If non-empty, used as the OTLP metrics endpoint. Otherwise falls back to `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`, then to `http://<DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL host>:4317/4318` based on protocol. For `http/protobuf`, `/v1/metrics` is appended if missing.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_HEADERS
configurations.description: Sets additional headers to include in OTLP exporter requests for metrics. When set, it takes precedence over OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS for metrics export.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_PROTOCOL
configurations.description: Specifies the OTLP transport protocol to use for metrics data. Takes precedence over the general `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL`. **Accepted values**: `grpc`, `http/protobuf`, `http/json`.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_TEMPORALITY_PREFERENCE
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies the aggregation `temporality` to use for each instrument kind. **Notes**: This default value `delta` is Datadog's recommended configuration and differs from the OpenTelemetry specification's default.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies the timeout (in milliseconds) for a single outgoing OTLP metrics request. Takes precedence over the general OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TIMEOUT. **Default**: 10000 (10s).

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies the transport protocol to use for all signals unless overridden.

configurations.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the request timeout for OTLP export (fallback for metrics-specific timeout). Used when the metrics-specific OTLP timeout is not set. Default value is 10000ms.

configurations.name: OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_HTTP_CLIENT_CAPTURE_REQUEST_HEADERS
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of HTTP header names. HTTP client instrumentations capture HTTP request header values for all configured header names **Notes**: Header tagging configured using OTel environment variables follows the OTel tag name convention of `http.request.header.<header-name>` rather than the Datadog convention of `http.request.headers.<header-name>`

configurations.name: OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_HTTP_CLIENT_CAPTURE_RESPONSE_HEADERS
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of HTTP header names. HTTP client instrumentations capture HTTP response header values for all configured header names **Notes**: Header tagging configured using OTel environment variables follows the OTel tag name convention of `http.response.header.<header-name>` rather than the Datadog convention of `http.response.headers.<header-name>`

configurations.name: OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_HTTP_SERVER_CAPTURE_REQUEST_HEADERS
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of HTTP header names. HTTP server instrumentations capture HTTP request header values for all configured header names **Notes**: Header tagging configured using OTel environment variables follows the OTel tag name convention of `http.request.header.<header-name>` rather than the Datadog convention of `http.request.headers.<header-name>`

configurations.name: OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_HTTP_SERVER_CAPTURE_RESPONSE_HEADERS
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of HTTP header names. HTTP server instrumentations capture HTTP response header values for all configured header names **Notes**: Header tagging configured using OTel environment variables follows the OTel tag name convention of `http.response.header.<header-name>` rather than the Datadog convention of `http.response.headers.<header-name>`

configurations.name: OTEL_JAVAAGENT_CONFIGURATION_FILE
configurations.description: Path to valid Java properties file which contains the agent configuration **Notes**: When OTEL_JAVAAGENT_CONFIGURATION_FILE and DD_TRACE_CONFIG are both set we apply the configuration from both files. This is an exception to the usual rule where the Datadog setting overrides the OTel one

configurations.name: OTEL_JAVAAGENT_EXTENSIONS
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of paths to extension jar files, or folders containing jar files. If pointing to a folder, every jar file in that folder is treated as a separate, independent extension.

configurations.name: OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER
configurations.description: Controls the OpenTelemetry logs exporter selection. This setting is not supported and is ignored.

configurations.name: OTEL_LOG_LEVEL
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the log level.

configurations.name: OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies the metrics exporter to be used. **Notes**: The only accepted values are `otlp` and `none`. A value of `none` disables the emission of OTel metrics, as well as APM runtime metrics (equivalent to `DD_RUNTIME_METRICS_ENABLED=false`)

configurations.name: OTEL_METRIC_EXPORT_INTERVAL
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies the time interval (in milliseconds) between metric export attempts. **Default**: 10000 (10s) **Notes**: This default value is Datadog's recommended configuration and differs from the OpenTelemetry specification's default of 60000ms.

configurations.name: OTEL_METRIC_EXPORT_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: **Description**: Specifies the maximum allowed time (in milliseconds) to collect and export metrics. **Default**: 7500 (7.5s) **Notes**: This default value is Datadog's recommended configuration and differs from the OpenTelemetry specification's default of 30000ms.

configurations.name: OTEL_PROPAGATORS
configurations.description: Specifies trace context propagation formats for both extraction and injection (comma-separated list). Lowest precedence; ignored if any other Datadog trace context propagation environment variable is set.

configurations.name: OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES
configurations.description: Sets OpenTelemetry resource attributes that are mapped to tracer tags when the corresponding tracer tag setting is not set. Reserved attributes for service, environment, and version are mapped to standard tags, and only the first 10 attributes are applied.

configurations.name: OTEL_SDK_DISABLED
configurations.description: Disables the Datadog SDK's OpenTelemetry interoperability for all signals. **Notes**: When set to `true`, this effectively sets `DD_TRACE_OTEL_ENABLED=false`, `DD_LOGS_OTEL_ENABLED=false`, and `DD_METRICS_OTEL_ENABLED=false`. **Ruby & Go SDKs**: The OpenTelemetry SDK activates automatically upon import and configuration, so this setting is not applicable.

configurations.name: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the application's default service name.

configurations.name: OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the exporter for traces. Only the value `none` is recognized, which is equivalent to setting trace export to disabled.

configurations.name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER
configurations.description: Sets the trace sampler via OpenTelemetry configuration and maps it to the tracer's sample rate when the corresponding setting is not set. Parent-based samplers are supported; unsupported values are ignored.

configurations.name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG
configurations.description: OpenTelemetry traces sampler argument (`otel.traces.sampler.arg` / `OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG`): used when `OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER=parentbased_traceidratio`; the value is treated as the traceidratio sample rate and mapped to Datadog's trace sample rate. Default: unset.
